entries.name: do-it-skills-index
entries.description: Generated compact index for lazy do-it skill discovery.

entries.name: do-it-router
entries.description: Use when starting any non-trivial repo task: pick Light / Standard / Heavy tier, then self-select meaning skills — not a fixed pipeline.

entries.name: do-it-code-quality
entries.description: Use when writing or designing code: name premise and blast radius, deepen modules at seams, TDD at agreed seams, diagnose before patching, and keep producer→consumer contracts honest.

entries.name: do-it-review
entries.description: Use when a diff needs PR-style review, or findings need atomic repair and same-scope re-review — Standards axis and Spec axis, kept separate.

entries.name: do-it-decide
entries.description: Use when premises or options must be pressure-tested, a short plan/handoff is needed, or a large plan must be sliced — grill facts locally, ask decisions one at a time.

entries.name: do-it-verify
entries.description: Use before any done, fixed, passing, ready, install, or merge claim, and when closing a branch — fresh evidence on this worktree; hooks vary by host, this skill is the checklist.

entries.name: do-it-retrospective
entries.description: Use only when the user explicitly asks to enable, disable, inspect, report, or persist opt-in local do-it behavioral feedback.

entries.name: do-it-context
entries.description: Use when repo terms or invariants are drifting, or when user/docs/code naming disagrees and a canonical glossary is needed — `.do-it/CONTEXT.md` is the downstream source of truth for names, relationships, and facts.

entries.name: do-it-handbook
entries.description: Use when a project needs a lean `.do-it/handbook/` for stable project truth plus `.do-it/worklog/` for daily or goal notes.

entries.name: do-it-skill-authoring
entries.description: Use when creating or updating do-it skills: write predictable model-invoked descriptions, progressive disclosure, leading words, and hook-aware escape behavior.

entries.name: architecture-strategist
entries.description: Use when an architectural choice or stage boundary needs a read-only view of invariants, ownership, extension seams, and proof.

entries.name: code-mapper
entries.description: Use when quick reconnaissance is complete and an unfamiliar or risky bounded scope needs a read-only trace or thorough map of ownership, contracts, consumers, and unknowns.

entries.name: code-quality-cleaner
entries.description: Use when a diff needs a read-only maintainability review for dead code, duplication, needless abstraction, reinvented primitives, and safe simplification.

entries.name: documentation-engineer
entries.description: Use when documentation must be updated to accurately reflect verified repository behavior, tooling, installation, or operator workflows.

entries.name: plan-challenger
entries.description: Use when a proposed plan needs a read-only challenge of outcome, scope, acceptance, risk, and cheaper credible alternatives.

entries.name: product-strategist
entries.description: Use when a feature or decision needs a read-only product lens on user goal, boundary, viable options, and tradeoffs.

entries.name: red-team-reviewer
entries.description: Use when a change or design needs a read-only adversarial review of trust boundaries, failures, state, persistence, concurrency, or replay.

entries.name: reviewer
entries.description: Use when a diff or delivered behavior needs a read-only correctness review for reachability, contract regressions, errors, and missing proof.

entries.name: spec-compliance-reviewer
entries.description: Use when a delivered change needs a read-only check against the written task, acceptance criteria, explicit deferrals, and ownership boundaries.

entries.name: tdd-red-writer
entries.description: Use when a behavior change benefits from a narrow failing test before implementation and a test-only write scope is available.
